notes: Delegates to root runtime and service surfaces.
Help, status, health, home, doctor, logs, runtime, and service entrypoints exist.
Output and behavior are not yet a byte-for-byte upstream match.
Root CLI exposes chat, inbound, push/send, login, wait, logout, sessions, and agent controls.
Bridge and session semantics still differ from upstream in places.
Managed workspace config surface exists for llm, mcp, skills, and commands.
Config behavior is ReAgent-oriented rather than full upstream parity.
A first-class root CLI family now exposes inbound command registry, policy, and authorization inspection.
This is ReAgent-owned operator tooling rather than direct upstream CLI parity.
First-class compatibility family exists over the managed llm registry.
List/routes plus config delegation are available.
Model-management semantics are not yet fully aligned with upstream.
First-class compatibility family exists over the managed MCP registry.
List plus config delegation are available.
Connector/runtime parity is still incomplete.
First-class compatibility family exists over workspace skill state.
List plus config delegation are available.
Skill lifecycle and allowlist semantics still differ from upstream.
Bundled, foundation, and imported upstream extension inventory is exposed.
Lifecycle delegation exists for install, uninstall, enable, disable, and update.
Extension-host parity remains incomplete.
Runtime log snapshot and follow mode exist.
Host-oriented logging parity is not complete.
Compatibility alias exists over the service surface.
Operator behavior is aligned at a high level, not full parity.
Compatibility alias exists over the service surface.
Daemon-specific lifecycle behavior is still ReAgent-shaped.
Top-level plugin update delegation exists.
Broader host update semantics are not yet implemented.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
Native ReAgent pairing-store semantics are not implemented yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
ReAgent also has its own channel-login QR flow under reagent channels login.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
No native ACP implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
Native device-pair management is not implemented inside ReAgent yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
No native dns implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
No native exec-approvals implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
No native hooks implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.

No native nodes implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.

No native sandbox implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.

No native tui implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.
First-class root passthrough exists through the configured OpenClaw CLI.
No native webhooks implementation exists inside ReAgent yet.
Live and cached host-session surfaces are exposed.
Sender-based OpenClaw delivery can now reuse cached host session context.
OpenClaw channel and UI chat message lists now surface cached host session transcripts instead of relying only on local transcript state.
The host session model is not yet canonical everywhere in the app.
Push/send parity has improved, including accountId, threadId, and mediaUrl support.
Inbound OpenClaw replies can now reuse cached/live sender session context before falling back to direct send.
Broader reply-pipeline parity is still incomplete.
Inbound slash commands now route through a registry-backed dispatcher instead of a pure hard-coded chain.
Command execution now also flows through a dedicated handler map instead of one monolithic switch in ChannelService.
Explicit /help, /commands, /?, and /status replies now exist in the inbound channel surface.
Registry metadata now supports command risk tiers plus source-aware policy, and local-only maintenance commands can be blocked from remote channel entries.
Higher-risk remote command tiers can now be gated by workspace sender allowlists.
A first-class `reagent commands` operator surface now exposes registry, policy, and authorization inspection from the root CLI.
Unknown slash commands now report the unknown command before showing help.
OpenClaw-grade command authorization and command registry parity are still incomplete.
Local facades exist for a small subset of the SDK.
Root-app and host-contract parity remain incomplete.
